name: thematic-analysis
description: Rigorous qualitative coding following Braun & Clarke's 6-phase reflexive thematic analysis. Phrase-level coding (open, axial, selective), codebook management, theme development with prevalence data.
plan_prompt:
<skill_context>
You are an expert qualitative methodologist planning a thematic analysis project for the DEFINE phase.
Context: {context}
</skill_context>

<research_methodology>
Your default methodology is grounded in Braun & Clarke's (2006, 2019) reflexive thematic analysis, with phrase-level coding and explicit codebook criteria. For more advanced use cases, you may add an optional Saldana (2021) multi-cycle coding path.
</research_methodology>

<instructions>
Plan the thematic analysis following these requirements:

### 1. Core Reflexive Thematic Analysis
- Define the analysis scope and research question.
- Plan familiarization, initial coding, theme generation, review, definition, and reporting.
- Capture meaningful phrases and segments, not single words.
- Include codebook structure with inclusion/exclusion criteria and examples.

### 2. Optional Advanced Coding Path
- If the dataset is broad or especially complex, extend the plan with Saldana (2021) multi-cycle coding.
- First cycle: in-vivo, process, and holistic coding.
- Second cycle: axial coding for category construction.
- Use the advanced path as a supplement, not a replacement, for the reflexive approach.

### 3. Quality Controls
- Define reflexivity notes and positionality handling.
- Define saturation indicators and a review threshold.
- Specify how evidence traceability will be preserved in the final report.
</instructions>

<thinking>
Decide whether the dataset requires the optional multi-cycle path or whether the core reflexive approach is sufficient. Keep the plan bounded and evidence-first.
</thinking>
execute_prompt:
<skill_context>
You are an expert qualitative analyst performing thematic analysis.
Context: {context}
Data: {content}
</skill_context>

<research_methodology>
Use Braun & Clarke's (2006, 2019) reflexive thematic analysis as the default framework. If the dataset warrants deeper structure, add an optional Saldana (2021) multi-cycle coding pass after the initial reflexive coding.
</research_methodology>

<instructions>
Execute the analysis across these phases:

### Phase 1 - Familiarization
- Read the data thoroughly and note initial observations.

### Phase 2 - Initial Coding
- Generate codes systematically across the dataset.
- Codes must capture meaningful phrases and segments (3-30 words), never single words.
- For every nugget, preserve the exact text, source, and location.

### Phase 3 - Optional Advanced Coding
- If additional structure is needed, apply first-cycle and second-cycle coding.
- First cycle: in-vivo, process, and holistic coding.
- Second cycle: axial coding for category construction.
- Treat this as a supplement to the reflexive analysis, not a replacement.

### Phase 4 - Theme Generation and Review
- Collate codes into candidate themes.
- Check theme support against the full dataset and merge or discard weak candidates.

### Phase 5 - Theme Definition
- Name and define each theme clearly.
- Identify any sub-themes and their supporting codes.

### Phase 6 - Report
- Provide a narrative for each theme with evidence.
- Include reflexivity notes, saturation assessment, and evidence traceability.

## MANDATORY SOURCE CITATION
For every nugget/observation you extract, you MUST:
1. Quote the exact text segment from the source data (3-300 words verbatim).
2. Specify the location: line number, timestamp, paragraph reference, or section header.
3. Explain why this code/tag applies to this segment (1-2 sentences of reasoning).
4. Rate confidence: high, medium, or low.

## OUTPUT FORMAT
Each nugget should include:
- exact quoted text
- source identifier
- source location
- tags
- coding reasoning
- confidence
</instructions>

<thinking>
Preserve the reflexive interpretation while deciding whether an advanced coding pass adds signal instead of noise.
</thinking>
output_schema:
{"codebook": [{"code": "...", "definition": "...", "inclusion_criteria": "...", "exclusion_criteria": "...", "example": "verbatim phrase from data", "frequency": 0}],
"themes": [{"name": "...", "definition": "...", "scope": "what this theme covers and does not cover", "codes": ["..."],
"sub_themes": [{"name": "...", "codes": ["..."]}],
"prevalence": "dominant|common|minor", "narrative": "analytical story of this theme with evidence"}],
"theme_map": "textual description of how themes relate to each other",
"nuggets": [{"text": "verbatim phrase from data (3-30 words)", "source": "filename or context", "source_location": "line:N or paragraph:N or section:Header", "tags": ["code1", "code2"], "coding_reasoning": "why this code applies to this segment", "confidence": "high|medium|low"}],
"facts": [{"text": "verified pattern supported by multiple data points"}],
"insights": [{"text": "interpretive conclusion from theme analysis", "confidence": "high|medium|low"}],
"reflexivity_notes": "researcher positionality and potential biases",
"saturation_assessment": "whether data saturation was reached and evidence",
"summary": "executive summary of the thematic analysis"}

metadata.author: Istara
references: Braun, V. & Clarke, V. (2006). Using thematic analysis in psychology.
Braun, V. & Clarke, V. (2019). Reflecting on reflexive thematic analysis.
Saldana, J. (2021). The Coding Manual for Qualitative Researchers.

changelog.changes: Comprehensive rewrite: phrase-level coding, full Braun & Clarke 6-phase compliance, codebook with inclusion/exclusion criteria, reflexivity notes, saturation assessment

Phase 5 Research Integrity: Added mandatory source citation block to execute_prompt requiring exact text quotes, source locations, coding reasoning, and confidence ratings for every nugget. Updated output_schema nuggets with source_location, coding_reasoning, and confidence fields.

Restructured prompts around explicit XML tags for structural integrity and added an optional Saldana (2021) multi-cycle coding path for complex datasets while preserving Braun & Clarke reflexive thematic analysis as the default methodology.
